CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, original_string VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, original_string) VALUES (5, 'nothing here');
SELECT id, original_string, strcasestr(original_string, 'abrA') AS found_index FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, original_string FROM test_strings WHERE strcasestr(original_string, 'AbrA') IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, original_string FROM test_strings WHERE strcasestr(original_string, 'notfound') IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE more_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO more_strings (id, description) VALUES (1, 'A B-Racada Bra');
SELECT s.id, s.original_string, m.description FROM test_strings s JOIN more_strings m ON strcasestr(s.original_string, m.description) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT m.id, m.description FROM more_strings m WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_strings s WHERE strcasestr(s.original_string, m.description) IS NOT NULL);
SELECT s.id, strcasestr(s.original_string, 'ABRA') AS start_index FROM test_strings s WHERE strcasestr(s.original_string, 'ABRA') > 0;
SELECT id, original_string, strcasestr(original_string, 'a') AS found_index FROM test_strings WHERE LENGTH(original_string) > 10;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE strcasestr(original_string, 'abrA') >= 0;
